headline: Meta launches cloud business, eases stock overhang
detail: Meta entering cloud is a positive catalyst for Meta itself and mega-cap tech broadly, which supports QQQ and SPY sentiment. However, it directly threatens AI infrastructure pure-plays - CoreWeave and similar names sold off hard on this news, and that sentiment can bleed into SMH and SOXL which hold semiconductor/AI infrastructure exposure.
rec: No new buys here. Watch whether SMH absorbs this or breaks lower - if chips weaken further, SOXL's stop at $200 becomes very real very fast. Hold what you have but tighten your mental stop on SOXL.

headline: CoreWeave, Nebius tumble on Meta cloud threat
detail: Direct AI infrastructure selloff today. This is the concrete expression of the Meta headline above - money is rotating away from AI picks-and-shovels names. SMH and SOXL both have semiconductor/AI infrastructure exposure and you're already down on both.
rec: This reinforces trimming or at least not adding to SOXL here. The 3X leverage means if semiconductors drop another 5%, SOXL drops ~15% - and you're already at -14.94% on margin. Consider reducing.

headline: Palantir's Karp bashes OpenAI and Anthropic token model
detail: Intra-AI-sector drama, but it signals continued volatility and narrative shifts in AI names. Not a direct holdings impact, but keeps AI sentiment choppy which is the air your entire book breathes.
rec: No action needed directly. Just reinforces that AI names are in a headline-driven environment - another reason not to be over-leveraged in this space, which you currently are via SOXL and SPAL.

headline: Dan Ives exits Wedbush for new venture
detail: Ives is one of the most widely followed tech bulls - his departure from Wedbush could reduce that firm's influence on tech sentiment temporarily. Not a price-moving event, but worth noting as a sentiment indicator for the retail tech community.
rec: No trade warranted. This is a personnel move, not a fundamental shift. Ignore and focus on your stops.
